Name: Lab.Oratory
Description:
Located in a former heating plant directly beneath the Berghain complex, Lab.oratory (Lab) is a dedicated men-only sex and fetish club. It operates independently from the main club upstairs and is not a standard dance venue.
=====
+++++ ENTRY & QUEUE
||Getting there||
Access the queue via Am Wriezener Bahnhof. The line forms down the alley.

||Choose the correct line||
The queue splits near the venue. The line for Lab turns left. The line on the right is for Berghain. Make sure you join the correct queue: the gate closest to the door is often locked, so you cannot cross between them.

||No internal Berghain access||
There is no internal access to Berghain through Lab. To visit the main club afterwards, leave Lab and join the regular Berghain queue.
=====
+++++ ENTRY & CHECK-IN
||Door times||
Entry is limited to a specific two-hour window at the start of the night, for example 20:00–22:00. Once the door closes, no further entry is allowed. People already waiting in the queue are generally processed.

||Wristband and bag system||
After entry and bag check, you receive a numbered wristband. This number is your bar tab for the night. You also receive a plastic bag marked with the same number for your street clothes at the cloakroom.

||Checking out||
Before leaving, collect your clothes and go to the counter to scan your wristband and pay your bar tab. You receive a receipt, which must be handed to door staff when exiting.
=====
+++++ INSIDE THE VENUE
||Atmosphere||
Lab is not designed for casual socialising, group hangouts, or conventional romantic behaviour.

||Play areas||
In the darkrooms and designated cruise areas, keep the atmosphere quiet and focused.
-- No talking or singing
-- No smoking
-- No phone lights
Vibe_Tags: Cruising, Darkroom, Men Only, Dresscode, Naked, Underwear, Fetish/Gear, Techno
Opening_Notes: Thursday from 21:00; Friday-Saturday from 22:00; Sunday from 16:00; entry windows vary by night

Description: Barnard Park is a small public green on Barnsbury Road in Islington. The current source describes it as a quieter local park, most relevant later in the day rather than a destination site.
Opening_Notes: Evening.

Name: Hampstead Heath
Native_Map_Query: Jack Straw's Castle, Hampstead Heath, London
Description: The public reference is West Heath by Jack Straw’s Castle and the white stone pond, not the whole Heath. A current London guide describes this side as the best-known outdoor area, especially around sunset and into the evening.
Opening_Notes: Sunset and evening.

Map_Points.Label: Hampstead Heath — West Heath / Jack Straw's Castle side

Name: Hyde Park — Rose Garden
Description: Use the Rose Garden and Rotten Row side of Hyde Park, with the nearby Serpentine as the wider public landmark. A current London guide describes this as a central outdoor area that is more active after dark, with Hyde Park Corner the nearest Tube reference.
Opening_Notes: Evening and after dark.

Map_Points.Label: Hyde Park — Rose Garden / Rotten Row side

Name: Kensal Green
Description: This small public park is directly opposite Kensal Green Underground and Overground station. The source describes it as a compact, exposed location that is more relevant after 7 pm than during the day.
Opening_Notes: After 7 pm.
